title: Character System — Player Stats
description: Create a Character class with name, char_class (warrior/mage/rogue), level, health, max_health, attack, defense, experience, and gold. Methods: gain_exp(amount) adds experience and calls level_up() when experience >= level * 100, level_up() increments level and boosts stats, is_alive() returns health > 0. Create a CharacterCreator with create(name, char_class) that applies class-specific base stats: warrior (health=120, attack=15, defense=12), mage (health=80, attack=20, defense=8), rogue (health=100, attack=18, defense=10).
test_code:
# Tests for Step 6: Character System
assert 'Character' in dir(), 'Character class must be defined'
assert 'CharacterCreator' in dir(), 'CharacterCreator class must be defined'

# Test Character constructor
char = Character('Hero', 'warrior', 120, 15, 12)
assert char.name == 'Hero', 'Character should store name'
assert char.char_class == 'warrior', 'Character should store char_class'
assert char.level == 1, 'Character should start at level 1'
assert char.health == 120, 'Character should store health'
assert char.max_health == 120, 'max_health should equal initial health'
assert char.attack == 15, 'Character should store attack'
assert char.defense == 12, 'Character should store defense'
assert char.experience == 0, 'Character should start with 0 experience'
assert char.gold == 0, 'Character should start with 0 gold'

# Test is_alive
assert char.is_alive() is True, 'Character with health > 0 should be alive'

# Test take_damage
char.take_damage(30)
assert char.health == 90, f'Health should be 90 after 30 damage, got {char.health}'

# Test take_damage doesn't go below 0
char.take_damage(200)
assert char.health == 0, 'Health should not go below 0'
assert char.is_alive() is False, 'Character with 0 health should not be alive'

# Test heal
char.heal(50)
assert char.health == 50, f'Health should be 50 after healing, got {char.health}'

# Test heal doesn't exceed max_health
char.heal(1000)
assert char.health == char.max_health, 'Health should not exceed max_health'

# Test gain_exp and level_up
char2 = Character('Mage', 'mage', 80, 20, 8)
assert char2.level == 1, 'Should start at level 1'

char2.gain_exp(100)  # Threshold for level 1 is 100
assert char2.level == 2, f'Should be level 2 after 100 exp, got {char2.level}'
assert char2.max_health == 90, f'max_health should be 90 after level up, got {char2.max_health}'
assert char2.health == 90, 'health should be restored to max on level up'
assert char2.attack == 22, f'attack should be 22 after level up, got {char2.attack}'
assert char2.defense == 9, f'defense should be 9 after level up, got {char2.defense}'

# Test multi-level gain
char3 = Character('Rogue', 'rogue', 100, 18, 10)
char3.gain_exp(300)  # Level 1 needs 100, level 2 needs 200 -> levels up to 2, then 300 >= 200
assert char3.level >= 2, f'Should be at least level 2 with 300 exp, got {char3.level}'

# Test CharacterCreator
creator = CharacterCreator()

warrior = creator.create('Tank', 'warrior')
assert isinstance(warrior, Character), 'create should return a Character'
assert warrior.name == 'Tank', 'Name should be Tank'
assert warrior.char_class == 'warrior', 'Class should be warrior'
assert warrior.health == 120, f'Warrior health should be 120, got {warrior.health}'
assert warrior.attack == 15, f'Warrior attack should be 15, got {warrior.attack}'
assert warrior.defense == 12, f'Warrior defense should be 12, got {warrior.defense}'

mage = creator.create('Wizard', 'mage')
assert mage.health == 80, f'Mage health should be 80, got {mage.health}'
assert mage.attack == 20, f'Mage attack should be 20, got {mage.attack}'
assert mage.defense == 8, f'Mage defense should be 8, got {mage.defense}'

rogue = creator.create('Shadow', 'rogue')
assert rogue.health == 100, f'Rogue health should be 100, got {rogue.health}'
assert rogue.attack == 18, f'Rogue attack should be 18, got {rogue.attack}'
assert rogue.defense == 10, f'Rogue defense should be 10, got {rogue.defense}'

# Test invalid class
try:
    creator.create('Bad', 'bard')
    assert False, 'Should raise ValueError for unknown class'
except ValueError:
    pass
acceptance_criteria: Character stores name, char_class, level, health, max_health, attack, defense, experience, gold
Character starts at level 1 with 0 experience and 0 gold
gain_exp() adds experience and triggers level_up() at threshold (level * 100)
level_up() increments level and boosts max_health +10, attack +2, defense +1, restores health
is_alive() returns True when health > 0, False otherwise
take_damage() reduces health to a minimum of 0; heal() restores up to max_health
CharacterCreator.create() returns a Character with class-specific base stats
CharacterCreator raises ValueError for unrecognized char_class
hints: In Character.__init__, set self.max_health = health and self.health = health.
In gain_exp, use 'while self.experience >= self.level * 100' to handle multi-level jumps.
Use max(0, ...) and min(self.max_health, ...) to clamp health values.
In CharacterCreator.create(), use: stats = self.CLASS_STATS.get(char_class) and raise ValueError if None.
